100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 4, 1926, American aviator Charles Lindbergh continued to bask in the spotlight following his historic transatlantic flight, further cementing his status as a national hero. In Europe, the League of Nations held discussions aimed at stabilizing post-war economies, while in Germany, political tensions flared as the government faced mounting pressure from various factions. Meanwhile, in the world of sports, the baseball season was heating up, with teams vying for position as summer approached.
